model.label: Claude Opus 4.6
model.description: Anthropic • Hybrid reasoning model that pushes the frontier for coding and AI agents, featuring a 1M context window.

pick.rationale: Purdue's historically elite offense—ranked #1 nationally with an AdjO of 131-132.6—gives them the edge in this compelling 2-vs-3 Sweet 16 matchup, as their roughly 2.3-point adjusted efficiency margin advantage (+30.5 vs +28.16) and Big Ten Tournament championship momentum (including an 80-72 win over #2 Michigan) reflect a team peaking at the right time. Gonzaga's elite defense (AdjD 93.9, 9th nationally) is the exact type of unit that could slow Purdue's attack, and the potential return of Braden Huff from a dislocated kneecap would further bolster the Bulldogs' frontcourt, making this a legitimate toss-up. However, Purdue's significantly tougher Big Ten schedule, superior overall efficiency profile, and the fact that even Gonzaga's defense is unlikely to fully contain the nation's most potent offense—while Purdue's mediocre defense (AdjD 100.5) should still be adequate against Gonzaga's 30th-ranked offense—tip the balance narrowly toward the Boilermakers.

reasoningStep.title: Gonzaga vs Purdue – Sweet 16 West Region
reasoningStep.summary: A closely matched 2-vs-3 seed battle pitting Purdue's #1 offense against Gonzaga's elite #9 defense. Purdue holds a slight overall efficiency edge (+30.5 vs +28.16) and enters with Big Ten Tournament championship momentum, while Gonzaga's defensive identity and potential Braden Huff return create a credible upset path. Purdue's Big Ten battle-testing and offensive ceiling narrowly win out.
evidence: Purdue KenPom #8 (AdjEM +30.5, AdjO 131.0 #2, AdjD 100.5); Torvik #8 (AdjOE 132.6 #1, AdjDE 100.2 #36)
Gonzaga KenPom #11 (AdjEM +28.16, AdjO 122.1 #30, AdjD 93.9); Torvik #11 (AdjOE 120.3 #37, AdjDE 94.0 #9)
Purdue won Big Ten Tournament championship beating #2 Michigan 80-72; Gonzaga 30-3 as WCC champions
Braden Huff (Gonzaga) out since Jan 15 with dislocated kneecap; reports suggest Sweet 16 return possible but uncertain and he'd be rusty
Purdue's defense flagged as primary vulnerability in multiple pre-tournament analyses; allows 70+ points per game
Gonzaga's elite defense is the matchup factor most likely to disrupt Purdue's offensive machine, but Purdue's offense has proven effective against top defenses all season

Gonzaga’s NCAA Tournament path only grows more treacherous the longer Braden Huff sits out | Dave Boling March 15, 2026 Updated Mon., March 16, 2026 at 3:44 p.m. X Email Reddit SMS By Dave Boling The Spokesman-Review My on-site scouting report on Gonzaga’s Braden Huff last week: Despite a stubborn knee injury suffered in January, Huff was still able to elevate and was dangerous at the rim. Visual evidence. Following the West Coast Conference Tournament title game, Huff was able to climb up a ladder and snip off a portion of the net at the rim with a pair of scissors. Beyond that? He was still unable to suit up and actually play. The GU star forward’s injured leg has already pushed past its estimated range of repair of between four and eight weeks. Entering the ninth week of recovery, Huff has been ruled out for the opening weekend of the tournament, starting Thursday at Portland’s Moda Center against No. 14-seed Kennesaw State . Their No. 3 seed, and opening weekend venue in Portland, is news that couldn’t be better for GU. But Huff’s absence affects the Zags’ future, and his doubt creates a bittersweet aftertaste during this time when celebration is the mode of the moment. Bitter? Obvious. Wondering what t

result.text: Purdue vs. Gonzaga Prediction, Odds, Picks - NCAA Tournament Sweet 16 | FOX Sports my favs Access and manage your favorites here DISMISS Scores Watch Betting WBC ⚾ FOX Super 6 💰 Stories Search Sign In Account SPORTS & TEAMS PLAYERS SHOWS PERSONALITIES TOPICS POPULAR SEARCHES - No Data Available - BROWSE BY SPORTS & TEAMS PLAYERS SHOWS PERSONALITIES TOPICS Built on Purdue vs. Gonzaga Prediction, Odds, Picks - NCAA Tournament Sweet 16 Updated Mar. 29, 2024 8:27 p.m. ET share facebook x reddit link Data Skrive A Sweet 16 matchup will feature the No. 1 seed Purdue Boilermakers (31-4) and the No. 5 seed Gonzaga Bulldogs (27-7) playing on Friday at Little Caesars Arena. The NCAA Tournament contest tips off at 7:39 PM ET. The Boilermakers enter their matchup against the Bulldogs as a 4.5-point favorite. The matchup features an over/under of 155.5. Continue reading to get everything you need to know before placing bets on the Purdue-Gonzaga clash. Purdue vs. Gonzaga Game Information & Odds When: Friday, March 29, 2024 at 7:39 PM ET Where: Little Caesars Arena in Detroit, Michigan How to Watch on TV: TBS Live boxscore on FOX Sports Boilermakers vs Bulldogs Betting Information Favorite Spread Favorite Spread Odds Underdog Spread Odds Total Over Total Odds Under Total Odds Favorite Moneyline Underdog Moneyline Boilermakers -4.5 -108 -112 155.5 -112 -108 -198 +164 Purdue vs. Gonzaga Prediction Pick ATS: Gonzaga (+4.5) Pick OU: Under (155.5) Prediction: Purdue 78, Gonzaga 75 Learn more about the Purdue Boilermakers vs. the Gonzaga Bulldogs game on FOX Sports! Purdue vs. Gonzaga Betting Insights Betting Line Implied Predictions Based on the spread and over/under, the implied score for the matchup is Boilermakers 80, Bulldogs 76. The Boilermakers have a 66.4% chance to claim victory in this contest per the moneyline's implied probability. The Bulldogs have a 37.9% implied probability to come out on top. Key Spread Facts Purdue is 15-14-2 ATS this season. Gonzaga is 15-16-0 ATS this year. Purdue has covered the spread 12 times this season (12-14-1 ATS) when playing as at least 4.5-point favorites. Gonzaga has covered the spread once when an underdog by 4.5 points or more this season (in two opportunities). Key Total Facts Boilermakers games have finished with more than 155.5 points 12 times so far this year. Bulldogs games have gone over 155.5 points on 13 occasions this year. These teams score 168.9 points per game combined, higher than the over/under for this matchup by 13.4. Key Moneyline Facts Purdue has been the moneyline favorite 25 times this season. They've finished 21-4 in those games. This season, Gonzaga has won two out of the four games in which it has been the underdog. Purdue has a record of 19-4 when it has played as a moneyline favorite with odds of -198 or shorter (winning 82.6%). Gonzaga has played as an underdog of +164 or more once this season and lost that game. Purdue vs. Gonzaga: Recent Results Boilermakers vs Bulldogs Recent Games Date F

path only grows more treacherous the longer Braden Huff sits out | Dave Boling March 15, 2026 Updated Mon., March 16, 2026 at 3:44 p.m. X Email Reddit SMS By Dave Boling The Spokesman-Review My on-site scouting report on Gonzaga’s Braden Huff last week: Despite a stubborn knee injury suffered in January, Huff was still able to elevate and was dangerous at the rim. Visual evidence. Following the West Coast Conference Tournament title game, Huff was able to climb up a ladder and snip off a portion of the net at the rim with a pair of scissors. Beyond that? He was still unable to suit up and actually play. The GU star forward’s injured leg has already pushed past its estimated range of repair of between four and eight weeks. Entering the ninth week of recovery, Huff has been ruled out for the opening weekend of the tournament, starting Thursday at Portland’s Moda Center against No. 14-seed Kennesaw State . Their No. 3 seed, and opening weekend venue in Portland, is news that couldn’t be better for GU.       "conference": "SEC"
    }

}

Now let me synthesize the data I have:

**Gonzaga (3-seed, 30-3)**
- KenPom: #11, AdjEM +28.16, AdjO 122.1 (30th), AdjD 93.9 (elite, ~9th)
- Torvik: #11, AdjOE 120.3 (37th), AdjDE 94.0 (9th)
- Elite defense, solid but not spectacular offense
- Braden Huff (dislocated kneecap, out since Jan 15) — reports suggest Sweet 16 return is "on the table" but uncertain; even if he returns, he'd be rusty after 2+ months out
- WCC conference (weaker schedule)

**Purdue (2-seed, 26-8)**
- KenPom: #8, AdjEM +30.5, AdjO 131.0 (2nd), AdjD 100.5 (mediocre, ~36th)
- Torvik: #8, AdjOE 132.6 (1st!), AdjDE 100.2 (36th)
- #1 offense in the country, but well-documented defensive vulnerability
- Big Ten Tournament champions (beat Michigan in final)
- Big Ten conference (much stronger schedule)

**Key matchup dynamics:**
- Purdue's AdjEM (+30.5) is ~2.3 points higher than Gonzaga's (+28.16)
- The critical matchup: Gonzaga's elite defense (#9, AdjD 93.9) vs Purdue's #1 offense (AdjO 131-132.6). This is the irresistible force vs immovable object dynamic.
- Purdue's mediocre defense (AdjD 100.5) vs Gonzaga's solid but unspectacular offense (AdjO 120-122). Gonzaga should score efficiently but not at an elite level.
- Purdue's defense is the clear vulnerability — articles specifically flag this as their upset risk factor
- Gonzaga's defense is elite enough to potentially slow Purdue's offense, which is the key to an upset path
- Braden Huff's potential return could boost Gonzaga's frontcourt, but he'd be rusty

**Assessment:**
This is a genuinely competitive 2-vs-3 matchup. Purdue has the higher overall efficiency margin and the nation's best offense, plus Big Ten Tournament momentum and a tougher schedule. However, Gonzaga's elite defense is specifically the type of unit that can disrupt Purdue's offensive machine. Purdue's defensive weakness (100.5 AdjD) means Gonzaga should score efficiently enough to keep it close.

The ~2.3-point efficiency margin gap favors Purdue, and their Big Ten battle-testing vs Gonzaga's WCC schedule is a meaningful factor. Purdue's offense is historically elite and has proven it can score against top defenses (they beat Michigan). I'll lean Purdue but with moderate confidence given Gonzaga's defensive identity.
